$comment: Canonical Nostr wire contract for the ndisc/glmps FEED-NOTE channel — owner-authored commentary that points at a release, plus the contributor registry and per-note sign-off that gate a small curated feed. ndisc is the publisher; the glmps web viewers + ndisc.view are consumers. Source of truth: xjmzx/ndisc src-tauri/src/lib.rs (feed_event / publish_feed_note / unpublish_feed_note); the contract test `mod schema_feed_v1` pins ndisc's output to this file. Design rationale: schema/current-feed-2026-06-23.md.

authority.model: single owner key — the root of all trust in this channel
authority.note: Viewers trust this pubkey as the root: the registry (kind:30000) and every sign-off (kind:4550) only count when authored by it. The owner secret stays in the platform keychain (ndisc) — the prototype's GLMPS_NSEC env path is throwaway, not the production signer.

feed_note.kindRationale: Its OWN kind, deliberately NOT 31238 — that number is already taken by the labels.v1 manifest (KIND_LABELS). A distinct kind keeps per-kind subscriptions simple and avoids any d-tag discrimination between label manifests and feed notes. Decided 2026-06-23 (see current-feed-2026-06-23.md §1).
feed_note.content: The note body — plain text, the owner's words. May be an empty string when the note is carried entirely by its image(s).
feed_note.writtenBy: owner AND registered contributors (each signs with their own key)

d.note: Stable per-author note id. The glmps: prefix namespaces feed notes; dedupe keeps the latest created_at for a given (kind, pubkey, d).

a.note: Reference to the release this note is about. The viewer hydrates artist/title/year/cover from the referenced kind:31237 — the note copies NO release metadata. Optional: a note may stand alone (no release reference).

published_at.note: Unix seconds rendered as a string (NIP-23 convention). Falls back to the event created_at when absent. Sort key for the feed.

image.note: Photo URL(s), typically nostr.build. First = lead image; the rest form a gallery, order preserved.

r.note: External link(s) — label page, shop, bandcamp, etc.

alt.note: NIP-31 human-readable fallback so generic clients show something. Emitter sets it to the title, else the first ~80 chars of the body.

feed_note.rule: Consumers MUST require only `d` structurally and treat every other tag as optional. The note's release context comes from resolving `a` against a kind:31237 event — a note whose `a` resolves to no known release is shown with a 'release unavailable' affordance, never dropped.

d.note: Fixed identifier — exactly one registry per owner.

p.note: One pubkey (hex) per allowed contributor. Replaceable: each publish is the FULL list — publishing an empty list removes all contributors.

contributor_registry.rule: Owner-signed only. A registry authored by any other key is ignored. This is the 'who may contribute' list.

approval.content: Optional — may carry the approved event's JSON. The address+id in the tags are sufficient to gate; consumers need not parse content.

a.note: Addressable coordinate of the approved feed note.

e.note: Event id of the specific approved note.

p.note: Author pubkey (hex) of the approved note.

k.note: Kind of the approved event. Redundant with `a`; consumers may ignore.

approval.rule: Owner-signed only. This is the per-note 'blessed' flag for the curated mode. Revoke a sign-off with a NIP-09 kind:5 referencing the approval event.

a.note: Unpublish a feed note by its addressable coordinate. Honoured client-side.

e.note: Delete by event id — used to REVOKE a prior kind:4550 sign-off (reference the approval event's id).

deletion.rule: Consumers MUST apply deletions client-side. Some relays (e.g. Primal) do not enforce kind:5, so server-side filtering cannot be relied on. A kind:5 from the OWNER revokes owner content or sign-offs; a kind:5 from a contributor removes only that contributor's own note.

trustGate._comment: What a viewer computes over the raw event stream to decide which feed notes to show. All authority keys on the owner pubkey. Reference implementation: feed-resolve.mjs.

requireApproval.note: Curated mode. When true, a contributor note is shown only if it ALSO has a matching owner kind:4550 sign-off. When false, registry membership alone suffices (trusted mode).

steps: 1. Owner-authored feed notes (pubkey == owner) are ALWAYS shown.
2. Resolve the latest owner-signed kind:30000 (d=glmps:contributors) -> the set of allowed contributor pubkeys.
3. A contributor note is shown only if its author is in that set AND, when requireApproval, an owner kind:4550 approval exists for its address.
4. Dedupe by (kind, pubkey, d) keeping the latest created_at.
6. Sort by published_at (falling back to created_at), newest first.

release.note: A feed note's `a` tag references a release.v2 event; the viewer reads that event to hydrate artist/title/year/cover. The feed channel never writes kind:31237.

profile.note: Author display (name / nip05 / picture), as elsewhere in the suite. Never published by this channel.

subscription.note: Viewers add kind 31239 alongside the releases they already read, same author + relays. Existing 31238 (labels.v1) handling is untouched — the distinct kind means no discrimination is needed.

changePolicy: FROZEN 2026-06-23 — pinned by the `mod schema_feed_v1` contract test in ndisc. ADDITIVE optional tags that consumers may ignore are permitted without a version bump; any BREAKING change (renaming/removing tags, changing semantics, making an optional tag required, changing the kind) is a coordinated feed.v2 bump — add feed.v2.json and migrate consumers, never edit this file. When an additive amendment lands, re-vendor this file into the glmps + ndisc.view consumers and re-pin their SHA. Adopting or amending this contract is a coordinated suite wave, not a local-only change.
rollout: ndisc gains a `current` view (the feed authoring + curation + live-match surface; current-feed-2026-06-23.md). The prototype .mjs scripts hard-code FEED_KIND=31238 and must be ported with FEED_KIND=31239. glmps (×2) and ndisc.view add kind 31239 to their subscriptions; A/V- and feed-unaware older builds simply show no feed until upgraded.
